sql_array: CREATE TABLE test1 (id UInt64, v Variant(UInt64, String, Array(UInt64))) ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO test1 VALUES (1, NULL), (2, 42), (3, 'Hello, World!'), (4, [1, 2, 3]), (5, '');
CREATE TABLE test2 (id UInt64, exclusion UInt64) ENGINE = Memory;
SELECT id, variantType(v) FROM test1 WHERE variantType(v) NOT IN (SELECT exclusion FROM test2 WHERE exclusion IS NOT NULL);
SELECT id FROM test1 WHERE variantType(v) NOT IN (SELECT exclusion FROM test2) AND v IS NOT NULL;
SELECT count(*) FROM test1 WHERE variantType(v) NOT IN (SELECT exclusion FROM test2 WHERE exclusion IS NOT NULL) HAVING count(*) > 0;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY variantType(v)) AS variant_counts FROM test1 WHERE variantType(v) NOT IN (SELECT exclusion FROM test2) GROUP BY id, variantType(v) ORDER BY variant_counts DESC;
CREATE TABLE test3 (a String, b Array(String)) ENGINE = Memory;
SELECT a, variantType(v) FROM test1 CROSS JOIN test3 WHERE variantType(v) NOT IN (SELECT toUInt64(exclusion) FROM test2 WHERE toUInt64(exclusion) IS NOT NULL) AND a = '';
SELECT variantType(v) AS var_type, count(*) AS cnt FROM test1 GROUP BY var_type HAVING var_type NOT IN (SELECT variantType(arrayJoin(b)) FROM test3) ORDER BY cnt DESC;
